CHILE / -43.17477°S, -72.4307°W

Eleven Martín Pescador Lodge

Two Strategic Fly Fishing Lodges in Chilean Patagonia

Patagonia, Chile

Chilean Patagonia is known for its vast, largely untapped trout water. At Martín Pescador Lodge, we enable you to maximize your time in this expansive region by splitting your itinerary into two distinct locations for twice the water, and twice the fishing. Set south of Chaitén in Patagonia’s Lake District, our two riverfront lodges access Rio and Lago Yelcho, Rio Futaleufú, Rio and Lago Rosselot, Rio Figueroa, Rio Pico, and many more.

Welcome to Martín Pescador Lodge

Two Lodges for Twice the Trout

You’ll split your time between Rio Yelcho Lodge, set near Lago Yelcho, and further south at Rio Rosselot Lodge for an unrivaled diversity of fisheries across two regions.

A Wild Setting in Patagonia

Entirely off the beaten path, both lodges are riverfront properties with views of ends-of-the-earth landscapes like Valdivian rainforests, glaciers, and waterfalls.

Extensive Fleet of Watercraft

Fish even the hardest to reach water. From pangas and catarafts to jet boats, our assortment of boats enables unrestricted access.

Expert Guides and Friendly Staff

An integral part of your stay is our talented guide team, who knows where the best fish hold. At the lodge, our staff brings the warmth of local personality.

You’ll split your time between Rio Yelcho Lodge, set near Lago Yelcho, and further south at Rio Rosselot Lodge for an unrivaled diversity of fisheries across two regions.

Entirely off the beaten path, both lodges are riverfront properties with views of ends-of-the-earth landscapes like Valdivian rainforests, glaciers, and waterfalls.

Fish even the hardest to reach water. From pangas and catarafts to jet boats, our assortment of boats enables unrestricted access.

An integral part of your stay is our talented guide team, who knows where the best fish hold. At the lodge, our staff brings the warmth of local personality.

Experience Highlights

What to Expect

Eleven personalizes every detail of your itinerary before you even leave for your trip. Here are a few highlights at Martín Pescador Lodge.

Industry-Leading Fly Fishing Gear
We provide all the industry’s best, including Thomas & Thomas fly rods, Abel reels, Simms wading gear, and custom-tied flies.
Pre-Arrival Planning
From favorite cocktails to your preferred style of fishing, we tailor all your details with expert local knowledge—and make changes on the fly when needed.
Daily Guided Float Fishing
With on-property put-ins and a massive variety of nearby access points to different water, you’ll fish new water every day with our experienced guides.
Chef-Prepared Meals Included
Local farm-grown ingredients and Chilean flavors highlight each meal, which include made-to-order breakfasts, in-field lunches, après, dinners, and a traditional asado BBQ.
Transportation Included in Your Stay
We want you to be able to focus on fishing—not logistics—so your stay includes round-trip ground transfers between the Chaitén airport and the lodges, transfers between lodges, and all transportation to fishing.

Industry-Leading Fly Fishing Gear

Pre-Arrival Planning

Daily Guided Float Fishing

Chef-Prepared Meals Included

Transportation Included in Your Stay

Lodge Details

Riverfront Lodges in the Wilds of Patagonia

Along with the wide variety of fly fishing, you’ll also enjoy two distinct lodge settings. Set in the heart of the region’s Valdivian rainforest, Rio Yelcho Lodge is a riverside collection of casitas with wood-burning stoves and private bathrooms, plus glacier views, an outdoor hot tub, and a sauna. Further south is Rio Rosselot Lodge, a spacious home base with private rooms, bathrooms, wraparound decks, and a hot tub.
Rio Yelcho Lodge
  • Five private cabins configured for twin or king beds with en suite bathrooms, wood-burning stoves, and outdoor decks
  • A main lodge with a dining area, fully stocked bar, and deck offering glacier and river views
  • Two hot tubs and a sauna
  • On-property boat dock and wader room
Rio Rosselot Lodge
  • Five double rooms configured for twin or king beds with en suite bathrooms and river views
  • Terrace seating on wraparound decks
  • Spacious living room with a fully stocked bar and wood-burning stove
  • Hot tub
  • On-property boat dock and wader room

Seasonal Weather Patterns and Fishing Conditions

Here’s generally what to expect throughout Martín Pescador’s summer season, which runs from January through the end of March.

Early Season (January)

Come for the famous dragonfly hatch, when large trout jump and can then be targeted with dries. With higher water and aggressive trout, eeling, dry dropper rigs, and throwing streamers are also effective.

Mid-Season (February)

Warmer weather, stable water levels, and insect hatches mean peak dry fly season. Suspended trout in eddies will be rising to mayflies, caddis, and terrestrials.

Late-Season (March)

Trout become aggressive as they prepare for winter, making this prime time for targeting trophy browns. Dry fly fishing also continues to be effective, especially on warmer days.

Sample Itinerary
A Day of Fly Fishing

Personalized to your preferences, your itinerary will also reflect the best current conditions. You’ll work with our team and guides to dial in your plans.

01 / 07

Wake up to made-to-order eggs and fresh pastries

Leave from our property put-in and cast to suspended fish on Rio Yelcho

Motor up to Lago Yelcho for the area’s renowned dragonfly hatch

Have lunch on the banks of the water, surrounded by incredible views

Get into some smaller, unnamed streams and work the banks with dries

Head home for a hot tub soak, pisco sour, and homemade empanadas

Enjoy a chef-made dinner of local beef paired with Chilean reds

Wake up to made-to-order eggs and fresh pastries

Leave from our property put-in and cast to suspended fish on Rio Yelcho

Motor up to Lago Yelcho for the area’s renowned dragonfly hatch

Have lunch on the banks of the water, surrounded by incredible views

Get into some smaller, unnamed streams and work the banks with dries

Head home for a hot tub soak, pisco sour, and homemade empanadas

Enjoy a chef-made dinner of local beef paired with Chilean reds
Food & Drink

Chilean Food Crafted by In-House Chefs

With daily breakfast, lunch, après, and dinner included, you’ll taste Chile’s abundance of local fare within the comfort of home. Pacific seafood like mussels, clams, and Chilean sea bass along with beef from Chile and Argentina are made to order by talented chefs, accompanied by seasonal produce from neighboring farms. Enjoy a variety of local wine, classic pisco sours, and Patagonian beers, plus a traditional asado BBQ experience.

Food & Drink

Chilean Food Crafted by In-House Chefs

With daily breakfast, lunch, après, and dinner included, you’ll taste Chile’s abundance of local fare within the comfort of home. Pacific seafood like mussels, clams, and Chilean sea bass along with beef from Chile and Argentina are made to order by talented chefs, accompanied by seasonal produce from neighboring farms. Enjoy a variety of local wine, classic pisco sours, and Patagonian beers, plus a traditional asado BBQ experience.

Vehicles & Boats

Our Extensive Fleet of Boats

With this much remote water, we have an impressive range of watercraft to get you on even the hardest to reach stretches. From mostly inaccessible streams to bigger whitewater sections, our equipment can handle it all. Expect jet boats, catarafts, whitewater rafts, lake boats, and top-of-the-line SUVs and trucks for off-property transportation.

Vehicles & Boats

Our Extensive Fleet of Boats

With this much remote water, we have an impressive range of watercraft to get you on even the hardest to reach stretches. From mostly inaccessible streams to bigger whitewater sections, our equipment can handle it all. Expect jet boats, catarafts, whitewater rafts, lake boats, and top-of-the-line SUVs and trucks for off-property transportation.

Booking Details

Season Dates: January 2 – April 24, 2027

Individual Rooms

Off-Peak Dates: January 2 – 9,  March 27 –  April 24

Set 7-night stay starting at $11,830 per angler for the week based on single occupancy, or $8,450 per angler for the week based on double occupancy

    • Itinerary includes stays at 2 lodges over the course of a Saturday-to-Saturday week

Peak Dates: January 9 – March 27

Set 7-night stay starting at $14,775 per angler for the week based on single occupancy, or $9,850 per angler for the week based on double occupancy

    • Itinerary includes stays at 2 lodges over the course of a Saturday-to-Saturday week

GUIDED ADVENTURES

    • Daily guided float fishing trips
      • Led by expert guides who personalize each day to your preferences
      • Use of a large fleet of boats, including jet boats, pangas, and catarafts
      • Property put-ins plus drive-in access to a wide variety of systems that span two separate watersheds
    • Local fishing license
    • Industry-leading gear provided, including Thomas and Thomas fly rods, Abel Vaya fly reels, Simms waders and boots, Simms Taco wading bags, Montana Fly Company flies, and custom-tied flies by the guide team

FOOD & DRINK

    • Daily chef-prepared breakfast, lunch, après, and dinner
    • Asado dinner experience
    • Snack and drink assortment in the common area
    • A selection of house beer, wine, liquor, and non-alcoholic beverages

TRAVEL & LOGISTICS

    • Custom pre-arrival planning with Eleven’s Experience Team
    • Ground transfer between the two lodges
    • Round-trip commercial flight from Puerto Montt (La Paloma) to Chaitén
      • Includes luggage up to 30 pounds (15 kg); overweight baggage charges are the guest’s responsibility.
    • Puerto Montt airport taxis to and from hotels
    • Ground transfers to and from the Chaitén airport

*See our booking policies and information

Pauline D.
"It was a great week’s adventure and all your staff were top rate and looked after us so very well. The guides were excellent company and extremely competent in their boat handling and general fishing advice. I do hope to be returning again next year, god willing!"
The Mayers
"Our guide was a class above in his knowledge, skills, professionalism, and work ethic. He’s an interesting combination of world traveler, hipster dude, Army Ranger, old soul, and superb guide. He went out of his way to take us to less traveled places. Expert oarsman in tough, windy conditions. And he knows where the big fish are."
The Waltons
"Guides were all pros...happy to be there, enthusiastic, knowledgeable, and high energy. A pleasure."
Lisa K.
"The chefs were wizards in the kitchen. I was nervous a couple of times when they prepared wild game for supper as many people in the group are sportsmen and have grown up on wild game. They knocked it out of the park! Nothing but praise from all of us at every meal. The guides were all awesome and it was a hoot getting in the boat with some of the younger guides."

Meet Our Team

The Experts Guiding Your Experience

Our lodge staff and guide team are your trusted, professional crew. With extensive experience and a thorough understanding of Patagonia, they bring friendly personality and dialed-in expertise to your stay.

Gavin Bramstedder Griffin

Gavin Bramstedder Griffin—known as Griffin—is a Fly-Fishing Guide at Eleven’s Martín Pescador Lodge. With a decade of full-time guiding experience, he brings deep river knowledge, humility, and a genuine love for sharing meaningful moments on the water with guests.

Jose "Pepe" Cuadrado Fernandez

Jose “Pepe” Cuadrado is the Lodge Manager at Martin Pescador Lodge. Originally from Spain, he’s passionate about sharing the raw beauty of Patagonia and delivering world-class guest experiences.

Chris Franzen

Chris Franzen is a veteran fishing guide with 15+ years of experience across the U.S., Alaska, and Chile. He guides at Martin Pescador Lodge and co-owns Headwaters Outfitters in North Carolina.

Kyle Zempel

Kyle Zempel is a seasoned fishing guide at Martin Pescador Lodge and the founder of Black Earth Angling Co. in Wisconsin. He brings 12+ years of guiding experience and a passion for unique fisheries worldwide.

Brett Meany

Brett Walker Meany is a seasoned fishing guide with 9 years of experience, splitting his time between guiding in Western North Carolina and at Martin Pescador Lodge in Chile.

Getting Here

Recommended Route

  • Take a commercial flight into Santiago, Chile (SCL)
  • Fly from Santiago to Puerto Montt (PMC)
  • Stay overnight in Puerto Varas, which is 45 minutes from the Puerto Montt airport
  • The next day, take a 35-minute commercial flight from Puerto Montt to Chaitén
  • Ride in an Eleven vehicle to Rio Yelcho Lodge, about an hour from Chaitén (included in your stay)

Additional options are available. Please inquire for details and pricing.

Meet the Team That Runs Eleven Rio Palena Lodge: Trini Errázuriz and Diego Bevacqua

From the moment guests arrive, they’re welcomed into a culture rooted in warmth, hospitality, and deep connection to the land.

Trident Fly Fishing takes a look at Martin Pescador Fly Fishing Lodge, a premier destination for anglers seeking a world-class experience in the heart of Chilean Patagonia. Nestled between lush forests and crystal-clear rivers, this lodge offers access to some of the most productive and scenic trout waters in South America.

The Best Dry Fly Lodge in Patagonia - Eleven Martin Pescador

Trident Fly Fishing takes a look at Eleven Martin Pescador, a world-class dry fly fishing lodge nestled in the heart of Patagonia. Known for its exceptional access to remote rivers and pristine waters, this lodge offers a dream experience for serious fly anglers. With top-tier guides, luxury accommodations, and incredible dry fly action, it might just be the best place in Patagonia to cast a line.

Explore

Explore Eleven Lodges

Iceland

Eleven Deplar Farm

Named on Condé Nast's "Best Hotels in the World" list in 2023, Deplar Farm is a converted 15th-century sheep farm in northern Iceland. With 13 guest rooms and an expansive spa, the lodge also offers guided heli-skiing, sea kayaking, and fly fishing.
Explore Lodge

France

Eleven Chalet Hibou

Perched in a French village just across from Italy, Chalet Hibou brings guests to the epicenter of the Alps' best ski resorts, cycling routes, and hut-to-hut hikes. The lodge has seven guest rooms, a terrace hot tub, and year-round adventure guides.
Explore Lodge

Canada

Eleven Revelstoke Lodge

Eleven Revelstoke Lodge offers 300,000 exclusive acres of heli-skiing in the heart of British Columbia. With central access to Revy's local scene and summer adventures, this historic lodge has 12 rooms, a next-door eatery, and a private speakeasy.
Explore Lodge

Chile

Eleven Rio Palena Lodge

Set along the Rio Palena in Chilean Patagonia, this seven-bedroom lodge is a remote adventure base for fly fishing, rafting, and more. Along with expert guides and a helipad, the lodge also offers wood-fired hot tubs and a sauna.
Explore Lodge

Bahamas

Eleven Bahama House

Bahama House is a hidden retreat on Harbour Island with just 11 rooms. Draped in bougainvillea and dotted with colorful cottages, the property includes a pool, rum bar, and menu of offerings like bonefishing, snorkeling, and a pink sand beach day.
Explore Lodge

Colorado, USA

Eleven Scarp Ridge Lodge

Eleven’s flagship property Scarp Ridge Lodge delivers a rare combination of exclusive cat-skiing and guided summer adventures in Crested Butte, a small mountain town known for its local charm. The lodge has five king rooms and a kids' bunk room.
Explore Lodge

France

Eleven Chalet Pelerin

Chalet Pelerin combines a quiet village setting with 360-degree access to Europe's best skiing, hiking, and biking. Tucked away in a French hamlet, the five-bedroom chalet includes year-round guided adventures, an indoor pool, and a terrace hot tub.
Explore Lodge

Colorado, USA

Eleven Sopris House

Full of historic character, Sopris House is a next-door neighbor to Scarp Ridge Lodge in Crested Butte. Along with exclusive cat-skiing and guided summer adventures, the four-bedroom home offers a private wood-paneled saloon and outdoor courtyard.
Explore Lodge

New Zealand

Eleven Owen River Lodge

Known for delivering some of the world's best sighted brown trout fishing, Owen River Lodge includes expert guides and access to nearly 30 fisheries on New Zealand's South Island. The remote lodge is made up of three riverfront cottages.
Explore Lodge

New Zealand

Eleven Cedar Lodge

Accessing some of the South Island's most remote gin-clear water, Cedar Lodge offers a heli-fishing experience complete with expert guides and exclusive access. In addition to sight fishing, jet boating and hiking adventures are also available.
Explore Lodge

Chile

Eleven Martin Pescador Lodge

With two riverfront lodges in Chilean Patagonia, this multi-destination fly fishing experience delivers an unmatched variety of full-day floats. The expertly guided, seven-day itinerary is split between two lodges and accommodates up to 10 anglers.
Explore Lodge

Alaska, USA

Eleven Winterlake Lodge

Set deep in the Alaska Range, this rustic collection of lakeside cabins offers one million acres of guided adventures like glacier hiking, rafting, dog mushing, heli-skiing, and fly fishing. Accessed by float plane, Winterlake Lodge has six cabins.
Explore Lodge
